items.content:

items.title: AP Interview: Zelenskyy says excluding Ukraine from US-Russia talks about war is 'very dangerous'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says that excluding his country from talks between the U.S. and Russia about the war in Ukraine would be “very dangerous.” Speaking on Saturday in an exclusive interview with The Associated Press, Zelenskyy also asked for more discussions between Kyiv and Washington to develop a plan for a ceasefire. (AP video by Srdjan Nedeljkovic). Read more: https://bit.ly/40Wz6Pa 

#ukraine #russia #news #zelenskyy

Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Israel PM welcomes release of hostages and urges next one is carried out in same 'safe manner'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu welcomed the release of three hostages as part of the ceasefire agreement with Hamas militants and said he expected the next release to take place in a similar "safe manner."

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Gaza ceasefire sees smoothest exchange yet of Israel hostages and Palestinian prisoners; AP explains
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Three men held hostage in the Gaza Strip for more than 15 months were freed Saturday, bringing to 18 the number of captives released by militants since the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas began Jan. 19. In the ceasefire’s first phase, a total of 33 hostages in Gaza will be released in exchange for almost 2,000 Palestinian prisoners held by Israel.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Three hostages reunited with their families in Israel
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Keith Siegel, Yarden Bibas and Ofer Kalderon were all reunited with family members across Israel after being freed in the fourth round of exchanges during the Gaza ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Arizona appoints first Latina and Black justice to state Supreme Court
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Arizona Democratic Gov. Katie Hobbs has appointed Maria Elena Cruz to the Arizona Supreme Court. The state appellate judge from rural Yuma County will become the first Latina and the first Black person chosen for the state’s high court.

#shorts #arizona #supremecourt</article>

items.title: Serbia protesters set to blockade Danube bridges to mark 3 months since canopy collapse killed 15
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Many in Serbia believe that the collapse was essentially caused by government corruption in a large infrastructure project with Chinese state companies. Critics believe graft led to a sloppy job during the reconstruction of the Novi Sad train station, poor oversight and disrespect of existing safety regulations. The issue has come to symbolize a wider discontent over the rule of law in Serbia.

(AP video shot by Vojislav Stjepanovic)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Italian navy ship takes back 43 migrants after third failed attempt to process them in Albania
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>An Italian navy ship Saturday took back to Italy the 43 migrants from the asylum processing centers in Albania following a court decision in Rome in the third failed attempt by Giorgia Meloni’s far-right government to process migrants in the nearby Western Balkan country.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Released Palestinian prisoners greeted in West Bank with cheers and tears
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>25 of the more than 180 Palestinian prisoners freed on Saturday arrived in the occupied West Bank. all were serving long or life sentences in prison, many for involvement in attacks against Israelis. They were greeted with cheers of welcome and tears of joy as they stepped off the Red Cross bus into jostling crowds of well-wishers in the Palestinian town of Betunia, near Ramallah. (AP video by Amer Abdeen; Produced by Jalal Bwaitel). Read more: https://bit.ly/3WINdFf 

#news #gaza #israel #hamas

Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Aftermath of Russian drone and missile attacks in Ukraine; at least 8 dead
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Several people died overnight as Russian drone and missile strikes pounded Ukraine’s towns and cities, local officials said Saturday. The attack prompted emergency power grid shutdowns in seven Ukrainian regions, including Poltava, state energy company Ukrenergo said. Read more: https://bit.ly/3QldAOh 

#ukraine #russia #news 
Source: Ukrainian Emergency Service

items.title: Sick and wounded Palestinian children prepare for evacuation under Gaza ceasefire deal
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A group of 50 sick and wounded Palestinian children were set to cross to Egypt for treatment through Gaza’s Rafah crossing on Saturday, the first opening of the border since Israel captured it nearly nine months ago. The reopening of the Rafah crossing represents a significant breakthrough that bolsters the ceasefire deal Israel and Hamas agreed to earlier this month.

#gaza #rafah #news #israel #palestinian

Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: FDA approves painkiller designed to eliminate the risk of addiction associated with opioids
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Federal officials on Thursday approved a new type of pain pill designed to eliminate the risks of addiction and overdose associated with opioid medications like Vicodin and OxyContin.

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration said it approved Vertex Pharmaceuticals’ Journavx for short-term pain that often follows surgery or injuries. Read more: https://bit.ly/42wp9t7 

#fda #oxycontin</article>

items.content:

items.title: Panama's president says there will be no negotiation about ownership of canal
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Panama President José Raúl Mulino said Thursday there will be no negotiation with the United States over ownership of the Panama Canal, and he hopes U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s upcoming visit will allow them to focus on shared interests including migration and combating drug trafficking. (AP/ Abraham Terán).

#panamacanal #news

items.title: What can a ‘black box’ tell us about a plane crash?
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>It’s one of the most important pieces of forensic evidence following a plane crash: The so-called “black box.” There are actually two of these remarkably sturdy devices: the cockpit voice recorder and the flight data recorder. And they’re typically orange, not black. (AP video: Kendria LaFleur). Read more: https://bit.ly/3WHQlBz 

#news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Morissette mourns for loss of Pacific Palisades

items.content:

The drills, focused on sharpening urban combat skills, were held in the Donetsk region and organized by the 108th Separate Mechanized Battalion, known as the Da Vinci Wolves, for their newly recruited troops. (AP Video by Vasilisa Stepanenko).

items.title: New Zealand mountain granted personhood, recognizing it as sacred for Māori
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A mountain in New Zealand considered an ancestor by Indigenous people was recognised as a legal person on Thursday after a new law granted it all the rights and responsibilities of a human being. Mount Taranaki — now known as Taranaki Maunga, its Māori name — is the latest natural feature to be granted personhood in New Zealand, which has ruled that a river and a stretch of sacred land are people before. Read more: https://bit.ly/3WFKIUu

#personhood #maori #newzealand #news

items.content:

Indonesia's foreign affairs ministry has since demanded a thorough investigation, specifically into the possible use of excessive force by the APMM. (AP video shot by Fadlan Syam). items.title: LIVE: Kash Patel’s confirmation hearing for FBI director

items.content:
<article>Watch live as Kash Patel, President Donald Trump’s nominee for FBI director, appears before the Judiciary Committee.

Patel spent several years as a Justice Department prosecutor before catching the Trump administration’s attention as a staffer on Capitol Hill who helped investigate the Russia probe. He has called for dramatically reducing the agency’s footprint, a perspective that sets him apart from earlier directors who have sought additional resources for the bureau.

items.title: LISTEN: Air traffic control audio as plane crashes at Reagan Airport
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A jet carrying 60 passengers and four crew members collided Wednesday with an Army helicopter while landing at Ronald Reagan National Airport near Washington, prompting a large search-and-rescue operation in the nearby Potomac River. Less than 30 seconds before the crash, an air traffic controller asks the helicopter if it has the arriving plane in sight: “PAT-25, do you have the CRJ in sight?” The controller makes another radio call to PAT25 moments later: “PAT-25 pass behind the CRJ.” Follow for live updates: https://bit.ly/4hEcEA9 

#planecrash #washington #usa #news

items.content:

Read more: https://bit.ly/4jDsfBB

#israelhamaswar #ceasefire #news #live</article>

items.title: Citing pope's pro-family view, couple at heart of Vatican bank ban on workplace marriage go to court
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>It has come as something of a surprise to many in the Holy See that the Vatican bank fired a young couple, with three young children between them, after a new internal bank regulation went into effect barring workplace marriages. The contradiction between what the pope preaches and what his Vatican practices isn’t lost on Silvia Carlucci and Domenico Fabiani, who on Thursday are challenging the ban in a wrongful termination lawsuit before the Vatican tribunal. Read more: https://bit.ly/3CgUjtW
(AP video shot by Paolo Lucariello and Maria Grazia Murru)

#pope #vatican #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: LIVE: Eight more hostages set to be released as Israel-Hamas ceasefire holds
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Watch live scenes from Jabaliya as Hamas is set to free three more Israeli hostages — two women and an 80-year-old man — as well as five Thai captives. Israel is set to release another 110 Palestinian prisoners. This is the third such exchange since a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip took hold earlier this month.  Read more: https://bit.ly/4jDsfBB

#israelhamaswar #ceasefire #news #live</article>

items.title: LIVE: Scene from Potomac River after plane crash at Ronald Reagan Airport
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Live scenes from Potomac River after a passenger jet collided with an Army helicopter while landing at Ronald Reagan National Airport near Washington, prompting a large search-and-rescue operation in the nearby Potomac River. Follow for live updates: https://bit.ly/4hEcEA9 

Credit: WJLA

#planecrash #washington #usa #live #news</article>

items.title: Congolese civilians caught in a growing rebellion flee to Rwanda after facing terrible choice
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Many of the millions of people trapped in eastern Congo's escalating rebellion face a terrible choice: Retreat into Congo's interior and seek the protection of a weak army in disarray, or cross into nearby Rwanda, which is accused of backing the rebels. Read more: https://bit.ly/3QgHLGl
(AP Video by Jackson Njehia and Brian Inganga)

#congo #rwanda #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: LIVE: Press conference after plane crash near Washington's Reagan Airport
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Live press conference after an American Airlines jet with 60 passengers and four crew members aboard collided with an Army Blackhawk helicopter while landing at Ronald Reagan National Airport near Washington. 

Inflatable rescue boats were launched into the Potomac River from a point near the airport along the George Washington Parkway, just north of the airport.  Follow for live updates: https://bit.ly/4hEcEA9

Credit: WJLA

#news #live #washington</article>
